# Transcrição: Facebook_Advertising_and_What's_Within_and_Outside_of_Your_Control

## [00:00 - 03:00]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer. Let's go! As a Facebook advertiser, there's a whole lot of stuff for you to worry about. But don't overcomplicate it. Simplify it. Focus on the things you can actually do something about. You have very little control over several things. Like the industry. If you work in the entertainment industry, you're going to have way more success and potential success than someone who's a plumber. Or the product. You could have a really crappy product that as an advertiser you're tasked to sell and your potential for success is just limited. Competition. You could be in a very competitive space, competitive time during the holidays. Industry. Or location. It's much more expensive in the US than it is in India, for example. There are other advertisers who are targeting the audience that you want to reach and it's going to cost more. What about brand recognition? Let's say no one trusts your brand or at least your client's brand that you're trying to run an ad for. You're immediately at a disadvantage. Built-in audiences like your website traffic, email list, social followers, they all give you a leg up. But if your client hasn't invested in these things and you have to focus only on cold audiences, you have to adjust your expectations. What about your landing page and website performance? Your ad as an advertiser just do an amazing job, couldn't do anything better. But you can't overcome a bad website or landing page. If it's unprofessional or doesn't function properly, you're not going to get the results. What about budget? You've got a client who just doesn't want to spend. A tight budget can limit your volume, can limit your optimization, can limit your impact. Now you can mitigate these issues the best you can. Set appropriate expectations. And then focus on what you can control. Things like your copy, creative, ad format, bidding, frequency, optimization, placements, targeting, your strategy. Within these decisions, there's a wide range of possible results. Now a good advertiser knows how to balance what is and isn't within your control. Attack it. This has been a pubcast shot. Guess what? Your questions can be answered as a part of a pubcast shot. Just submit it to me at infoatjohnlummer.com. Make sure you put in the subject line, pubcast shot question, and maybe, just maybe, I'll answer your question on a pubcast shot. Thanks for joining me again today.

## [03:00 - 03:05]

Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
